pulls.title: Multiple updates from Erik Nygren.
pulls.author: enygren
pulls.body:
- Change Proxy-DNS-SVCB-Request to take the RR type to resolve (HTTPS or SVCB) as well as an optional name= which is commonly needed for SVCB.  Update examples to show a mix of HTTPS RR and SVCB RR usage.

- Reintroduce the recommendation to include the Proxy-Status header field. (Future TODO: we should add an example for matching the next-hop-aliases to the TargetName.)

- Update description and examples for CONNECT-UDP to match RFC

- Explain how client can determine if a connection is reusable (ie, by looking at the TargetName).

- Rename Close-On/Closed-On to Cancel-On/Cancelled-On since the proxy didn't make (or shouldn't have made) a connection in the first place so there's nothing to close.  I also considered "Abort" and "Skip" but "Cancel" seemed both clear and to not have the Inclusive Language issues associated with "Abort".

- Added the "https" token for Proxy-DNS-Request-Cancel-On for cancelling port 80 connections when an HTTPS RR is present to enable the HSTS behavior. (This has the potential to be compelling use-case for some people.) Add an example for this case.

- Added multiple additional Security Considerations covering

- Added myself as a co-author

comments.author: tfpauly
comments.body: Rendered view: https://gakiwate.github.io/draft-gakiwate-dnsop-proxy-dns-svcb/enygren-proposed-edits-2026-07/draft-gakiwate-dnsop-proxy-dns-svcb.html

reviews.author: gakiwate
reviews.body:
Thanks for the PR and makes for a great draft-00. My suggestions are mostly nits and fixing typos.

I like the renaming to Cancel-On/Cancelled-On. We might have to update some language to be consistent with "cancelling" as opposed to "closing" around that but that is not urgent. 

Welcome to the draft! :)
